channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: TMC appoints Kalyan Banerjee as Lok Sabha chief whip amid organisational reshuffle
item.description: In a strategic move to stabilise party leadership after recent electoral challenges in West Bengal, former Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has appointed Kalyan Banerjee as the Trinamool Congress's new ...

item.title: How Nairobi’s Free Running Community is Changing City Life
item.description: On Saturday mornings, Nairobi streets, particularly around KOFISI Square, are filled with runners bright-eyed and eager, signalling a shift in the city’s lifestyle. What began in 2022 with Emily Chepk...

item.title: Anni-Frid Lyngstad's Early Career as a Singer in Sweden During the 1960s
item.description: Anni-Frid Lyngstad, known worldwide as Frida from the iconic pop group ABBA, began her musical journey long before international fame. Born in 1945, she started performing professionally at just 13 ye...

item.title: WBA Sanctions Oleksandr Usyk vs Rico Verhoeven Heavyweight Title Defence Bout in Egypt
item.description: The World Boxing Association (WBA) has officially sanctioned a heavyweight title defence for undisputed champion Oleksandr Usyk against former kickboxing star Rico Verhoeven. The crossover fight is sc...

item.title: Imam Abdul Halim Khan sentenced to life for sexual abuse of women and girls
item.description: Abdul Halim Khan, an imam, has been sentenced to life imprisonment for committing repeated sexual offences against women and girls. The court heard that Khan abused his position of trust within the co...

item.title: Ngina Kenyatta Launches Green Haven Restaurant on Family Tea Estate in Kiambu
item.description: Ngina Kenyatta, daughter of former President Uhuru Kenyatta, ventured into the hospitality and restaurant business in 2018 with the launch of Green Haven, a luxurious eatery situated on the Kenyatta f...

item.title: Domestic Milk, Meat, and Egg Consumption Rises as Tanzania Reduces Imports
item.description: In Tanzania, the government is seeing a positive trend in local food consumption, particularly in milk, meat, and eggs. The Minister for Livestock and Fisheries, Dr Bashiru Ally Kakurwa, revealed in P...

item.title: Paul McCartney reflects on seeing Bob Dylan live and differences in their performance styles
item.description: Paul McCartney has shared his experiences attending Bob Dylan concerts, admitting that even as a longtime fan, he sometimes found it difficult to recognise which songs Dylan was performing. Speaking o...

item.title: American man uses church records to trace wife's Canadian ancestry for citizenship
item.description: Greg Kearney, a cartoonist from Topeka, Kansas, used resources from the Church of Latter-Day Saints (LDS) to help his wife trace her Quebec ancestry and qualify for Canadian citizenship. While Kearney...

item.title: Southampton face disciplinary hearing for alleged spying ahead of Championship play-off final
item.description: Southampton are facing serious disciplinary action following allegations of espionage during the Championship play-offs. An intern at the club, William Salt, was reportedly caught filming Middlesbroug...

item.title: Diplomat notes imbalance in opening remarks between Trump and Xi during Beijing visit
item.description: During a recent visit to Beijing, former Ambassador Michael McFaul highlighted a notable asymmetry between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping in their opening remarks. Accord...

item.title: Iraq Requests Citizenship for Graham Arnold After Leading Team to World Cup
item.description: Former Socceroo and ex-Australian national coach Graham Arnold has been celebrated in Iraq after guiding the Iraqi national football team to their first FIFA World Cup in 40 years. Appointed as Iraq's...

item.title: Telangana DGP Inspects Greyhounds and OCTOPUS Forces to Boost Preparedness
item.description: On 14th May 2026, Telangana's Director General of Police, C.V. Anand, along with DGP Operations Anil Kumar and other senior Greyhounds officers, conducted a detailed review of the state’s elite forces...

item.title: Nigeria Minister Present Blue Economy Agenda and Achievements at Africa Forward Summit in Kenya
item.description: The Minister of Marine and Blue Economy, Adegboyega Oyetola, don represent Nigeria well for the Africa Forward Summit wey take place for Nairobi, Kenya. He join high-level panel discussion on blue eco...

item.title: Ian Strange Explores Suburban Life Through Transformative House Art
item.description: Over the last ten years, Australian artist Ian Strange has undertaken a series of bold interventions on residential structures across the United States, New Zealand, Australia, and Poland. Rather than...

item.title: IFC Chief Warns African SMEs of Financial and Succession Challenges
item.description: At the Africa CEO Forum in Kigali, International Finance Corporation (IFC) managing director Makhtar Diop delivered a strong message to African leaders and investors, emphasising the need to move from...

item.title: Election Commission defers SIR rollout in J&K, Ladakh and Himachal due to weather and operational challenges
item.description: The Election Commission of India (ECI) has announced the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls across 16 states and three Union Territories starting May 30, 2026. However, Jammu and Kash...

item.title: Groups urge Senate to hold fair impeachment trial for Vice President Sara Duterte
item.description: Several organizations, including the Parish Pastoral Council for Responsible Voting (PPCRV), the Makati Business Club, and other professional and civic groups, have called on the Philippine Senate to ...

item.title: Angus Taylor proposes linking migration levels to housing availability
item.description: Following the government’s budget announcement, opposition leader Angus Taylor has outlined his approach to addressing Australia’s housing crisis. Taylor has pledged to end what he terms 'mass migrati...

item.title: Nova Scotia Provides $10 Farmers’ Market Vouchers for Students and Newcomers
item.description: The government of Nova Scotia has reintroduced a program offering $10 vouchers to support access to local farmers’ markets for students and newcomers. This initiative aims to promote healthy eating ha...
